title: Prove the adapter seam with a minimal CLI surface
summary: After Pi migration is stable, add a minimal CLI adapter that calls the same application use cases to prove the agent-agnostic seam before larger MCP/Codex/Claude integrations.

goal.outcome: A second simple adapter validates that CodeWiki workflows are no longer Pi-bound.
acceptance: CLI exposes a minimal state read command and one task operation using application use cases.
CLI code does not duplicate domain/task/session logic from Pi tools.
Tests prove Pi and CLI paths share application behavior.
Findings from the CLI proof update future MCP/Codex/Claude adapter tasks if needed.
non_goals: Do not build a full CLI product.
Do not implement MCP server, Codex extension, or Claude Code integration in this task.
verification: Run minimal CLI command against a temp fixture if packaged.

delta.desired: A tiny second adapter proves the ports/application seam with minimal complexity.
delta.current: Only Pi is a real adapter. Application/core seams are improved, but no CLI adapter exists yet; missing future CLI path was removed from code_paths to avoid lint noise.
delta.closure: CLI proof works or records seam gaps as follow-up tasks.
